steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $-7x^{6}+4x^{3}-14x+14$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group by degree: $x^6$ term: $-7x^6$. $x^3$ term: $4x^3$. $x$ terms: $-5x - 9x = -14x$. Constants: $8 + 6 = 14$. Result: $$-7x^6 + 4x^3 - 14x + 14.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $-3x^9 - 14x - 14$. Wrong — you cannot combine $4x^3$ and $-7x^6$ into $-3x^9$ (different degrees), and the constant sign is wrong.
  • B: $-7x^6 + 4x^3 - 14x + 14$. Matches the simplified expression. Correct.
  • C: $-7x^6 + 4x^3 + 4x + 2$. Wrong — sign on $-9x$ and on $-(-6)$ were mishandled.
  • D: $-3x^9 - 5x + 2$. Wrong — same illegal exponent addition and arithmetic errors.
That confirms B is correct.
